text:
There was this girl named Mia who only read books. She lived alone in a house at the edge of a small forest, and every day she'd quietly wander among the trees, finding quiet corners where she'd curl up with a novel.

One night, as the stars blurred into a sea of stars, she read a tale about a wise owl who could see the future. Intrigued, Mia imagined herself as that owl, perched atop a ancient oak.

She dreamt of journeys through time, witnessing love stories unfold, tragedies fall, and heroic deeds. With each reading, Mia felt more alive, her spirit intertwined with the tales she cherished.

As the weeks went by, she began to forget her worries—work, bills, life's complexities seemed insignificant beside the adventures of the wise owl.

Finally, one quiet morning, she woke with a start. The stars weren't dimmed anymore—it was daytime. Panicked, she dashed outside to find a world she'd never seen during the
